LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. (CCP 430.10(E).) CAN-SPAM ACT DOES NOT PREEMPT BUS. & PROF. CODE 17529.5 (" 17529.5"). (BALSAM V. TRANCOS, INC. (2012) 203 CAL.APP.4TH 1083, 1102; HYPERTOUCH, INC. V. VALUECLICK, INC. (2011) 192 CAL.APP.4TH 805, 817.) PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A VIOLATION OF 17529.5. FOR HEADER INFORMATION PURSUANT TO 17529.5(A)(2), DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED THAT TWO EMAILS SENT BY DEFENDANT IN WHICH THE "SENDER DOMAIN NAME . . . NEITHER IDENTIFIES THE ACTUAL SENDER ON ITS FACE NOR IS READILY TRACEABLE TO THE SENDER USING A PUBLICLY AVAILABLE ONLINE DATABASE SUCH AS WHOIS." (BALSAM, SUPRA, 203 CAL.APP.4TH AT 1101. SEE MPA, P. 7:14-15.) PLAINTIFF NEED NOT ALLEGE A MISREPRESENTATION, RELIANCE OR DAMAGES. (HYPERTOUCH, INC., SUPRA, 192 CAL.APP.4TH AT 820, 821, 822.) FOR SUBJECT LINE INFORMATION PURSUANT TO 17529(A)(3), DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER DOES NOT ADDRESS THE ALLEGATIONS PERTAINING TO THIS VIOLATION. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT SPECIALLY DEMURS FOR UNCERTAINTY, THESE GROUNDS ARE NOT STATED IN THE DEMURRER. (MPA, P. 10:4-18; DEMURRER, P. 2:7-22.) THE PROFFERING OF SUPPORTING AND OPPOSING DECLARATIONS BY BOTH PARTIES IS IMPROPER AND NOT CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT THE PLEADING WAS FILED, BUT NOT TO THE FACTS ASSERTED THEREIN. COURT TO REVIEW PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JASON BISHOP, SHELLY CARPER, STEVEN CARPER, JULIE CHENDES AND DIANA HELLMAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE EMAILS THAT PLAINTIFFS SEEK TO ADD TO THE COMPLAINT WERE RECEIVED PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT, AND WERE THEREFORE NOT AVOIDABLE ON THE GROUNDS ARGUED BY DEFENDANT. MOREOVER, AS TRIAL HAS NOT YET BEEN SET, DEFENDANT WILL NOT SUFFER ANY PREJUDICE IN RESPONDING TO THESE ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS. FURTHERMORE, THE VALIDITY OF THE PROPOSED AMENDED PLEADING IS NOT GENERALLY CONSIDERED BY COURTS IN DECIDING WHETHER TO GRANT LEAVE TO AMEND, AND DEFENDANT'S PROFFERED GROUNDS FOR MOTION TO STRIKE ARE PREMATURE. (SEE KITTREDGE SPORTS CO. V. SUP.CT. (MARKER, U.S.A.) (1989) 213 CAL.APP.3D 1045, 1048.) FINALLY, DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR CONDITIONS UPON GRANTING LEAVE IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT ANY GOOD CAUSE TO POTENTIALLY DENY ANY PLAINTIFFS' STATUTORY RIGHTS.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JASON BISHOP, SHELLY CARPER, STEVEN CARPER, JULIE CHENDES and DIANA HELLMAN'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is granted. The emails that Plaintiffs seek to add to the Complaint were received prior to the filing of the original Complaint, and were therefore not avoidable on the grounds argued by Defendant. Moreover, as trial has not yet been set, Defendant will not suffer any prejudice in responding to these additional allegations. Furthermore, the validity of the proposed amended pleading is not generally considered by courts in deciding whether to grant leave to amend, and Defendant's proffered grounds for motion to strike are premature. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Marker, U.S.A.) (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) Finally, Defendant's request for conditions upon granting leave is denied. Defendant failed to present any good cause to potentially deny any Plaintiffs' statutory rights.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. DEMURRER TO AMENDED GENERAL DENIAL
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Balsam, Esq., 415.889.2873, for plaintiff Jason Bishop.
Leeor Neta, Esq., 415.944.5424, for defendant Consumertrack, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JASON BISHOP's DEMURRER TO AMENDED GENERAL DENIAL is overruled. Defendant has pled sufficient facts with the requisite particularity to allege the first, third - eighth affirmative defenses. The Court's ruling on Defendant's demurrer to the Complaint does not preclude Defendant from asserting different or contradictory facts to the Complaint. A lthough Bus. & Prof. Code 17529.5 is a strict liability statute, Defendant may reduce its liability. (See Hypertouch, Inc. v. ValueClick, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 822.) A demurrer to a prayer for relief for attorney fees is improper. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Motion To Strike Consumertracks Amended Answer
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Balsam, Esq., 415.889.2873, for plaintiff Jason Bishop.
Leeor Neta, Esq., 415.944.5424, for defendant Consumertrack, Inc.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JASON BISHOP's Motion To Strike Consumertracks Amended Answer is denied as to the first and fourth affirmative defenses. Granted, without leave to amend, as to the prayer for attorney fees. The motion to strike the first and fourth affirmative defenses is predicated on the same grounds as the demurrer that has been overruled. Defendant has not pled the statutory basis for attorney fees, and has not demonstrated that amendment is proper. To the extent that Defendant may seek relief for attorney fees pursuant to CCP 128.7, Defendant may file a motion for sanctions; it need not be sought in the Answer. Order signed in open court. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. (CCP 430.10(e).) CAN-SPAM Act does not preempt Bus. & Prof. Code 17529.5 (" 17529.5"). (Balsam v. Trancos, Inc. (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 1083, 1102; Hypertouch, Inc. v. ValueClick, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 817.) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a violation of 17529.5. For header information pursuant to 17529.5(a)(2), Defendant concedes that Plaintiff has alleged that two emails sent by Defendant in which the "sender domain name . . . neither identifies the actual sender on its face nor is readily traceable to the sender using a publicly available online database such as WHOIS." (Balsam, supra, 203 Cal.App.4th at 1101. See MPA, p. 7:14-15.) Plaintiff need not allege a misrepresentation, reliance or damages. (Hypertouch, Inc., supra, 192 Cal.App.4th at 820, 821, 822.) For subject line information pursuant to 17529(a)(3), Defendant's demurrer does not address the allegations pertaining to this violation. To the extent that Defendant specially demurs for uncertainty, these grounds are not stated in the demurrer. (MPA, p. 10:4-18; Demurrer, p. 2:7-22.) The proffering of supporting and opposing declarations by both parties is improper and not considered by the Court. Plaintiff's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted to the extent that the pleading was filed, but not to the facts asserted therein. Court to review proposed order submitted via email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
